info.title: Veengu Platform Frontend API
info.description:
# General info
  Veengu API provides key methods and attributes for managing a digital financial service by Tenants (service providers).
## Authentication
### Veengu Tenant code
Also as a part of tenant onboarding, Veengu assignes an unique string code for the Tenant. You should always send your code in `v-tenant` header.
#
Note your API keys carry many privileges, so be sure to keep them secure! Do not share your API keys in publicly accessible areas such as GitHub, client-side code, and so forth.
#
All API requests must be made over HTTPS. Calls made over plain HTTP will fail. API requests without authentication will also fail.

### Access Token structure
Veengu Access Token is JSON Web Token (JWT, [tools.ietf.org](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519)). It consists of three concatenated Base64url-encoded strings, separated by dots `.`:

* Header: contains metadata about the type of the token and cryptographic algorithms used to secure its contents.

* Payload: contains verifiable security statements, such as an identity of the user and permissions they are allowed.

* Signature: used to validate that the token is trustworthy and has not been tampered with.

## Request identifiers
Each API request has an associated request identifier. You can find this value in response headers, under `V-Request-Id`. If you need to contact us about a specific request, providing the request identifier will ensure the fastest possible resolution.
  
## Channel Identification
  
Each API request may specify the channel type via the `V-Api-Channel` header. This header is optional: if not provided, the system will automatically apply the default value of `MOBILE`. This identifier helps route requests appropriately and ensures proper handling based on the source platform.  The channel type is stored in transaction details and may affect notification delivery.
Channel code  |  Description
-----------|--------------
`MOBILE` | Mobile applications
`USSD` | USSD channel
`WEB` | Web applications
`WHATSAPP` | WhatsApp integration
`MIDDLEWARE` | External systems
`OTHER` | Other channels

## Fields special formats
### Date and time formats
All dates in the JSON payloads are represented in ISO 8601 date-time format. All date-time fields in responses must include the timezone.  You can find more information on Veengu Developer Guide [**API Overview**](https://veengu.com/api/).
To get transaction created date in customer local time you use `createdAt` attribute in transaction response and transaction history.

During transaction initiation you can define transaction local timezone on application client site with sending `localUTCOffset` attribute as an offset of user mobile device with the locale time zone in ISO 8601 as an offset from UTC formats:

  * ±hh:mm
  * ±hhmm
  * ±hh

  In case `localUTCOffset` attribute is not specified in transaction request Veengu processing core stores and responses `createdAt` value as server time with Tenant timezone defines in settings.

### Money format
All money in API defined as amount object with mandatory fields value and currency.
Currency should be present as currency code in ISO 4217 alpha-3 format.
The value field should be present as the float number of monetary units specified in an appropriate currency. Field pattern applies based on currency  ISO 4217 definition.
## Versioning
When we make backwards-incompatible changes to the API, we release new, dated versions. The current version is 2.X.Y. Read our API changelog to learn more about backwards compatibility.
Some versions could be deprecated which means you cannot choose this version in API settings and all requests return an API format error.
To set the API version for a specific request use V-Version header.
As a precaution, use API versioning to test a new API version before committing to an upgrade.

## Errors
For error handling Veengu uses conventional HTTP response codes to indicate success or failure of an API request. In general, error codes in the `2xx` range indicate success. Codes in the `4xx` range indicate errors that failed given the information provided (e.g., a required parameter is missing, a failed transaction, etc.). Codes in the `5xx` range indicate errors with Veengu servers (these are rare).
Some `4xx` errors that could be handled programmatically (e.g., a transaction is declined) include an error code that briefly explains the error reported.

HTTP code  |  description
-----------|--------------
200 | Request processed successfully.
201 | Request processed successfully.
400 | The request cannot be not accepted, often due to missing a required parameter.
401 | Not authenticated
402 | The parameters were valid but the request failed.
403 | The API key doesn't have permissions to perform the request.
404 | The requested resource does not exist.
409 | The request is conflicting with another request (perhaps due to using the same idempotent key)
429 | Too many requests hit the API too quickly. We recommend an exponential backoff of your requests.
500 | Something went wrong on Veengu's end. (These are rare.)
504 | Where an API is deprecated and no longer operationally supported by an Veengu.


## Pagination
The Veengu API implements cursor-based pagination via the `next` and `prev` parameters. Both parameters take a unique key element that identifies the next or previous page. The `prev` parameter returns objects listed before the current page. The `next` parameter returns objects listed after the current page.  Only one parameter `next` or `prev` should be used in the request. If both parameters are provided, only `next` is used.
  The returned object includes cursor object with `next`/`prev` parameters if there are more objects (for example, transactions) than the defined limit value in response. If the objects list fits in one page cursor object is empty.

All top-level API resources have support request to get object list via API methods. For instance, you can get customers, accounts, and transactions. These API methods share a common structure, taking at least these three parameters: `limit`, `next` and `prev`.

The API Specification and the Veengu Platform are protected by applicable intellectual property, copyright, and trade secret laws. 2. All intellectual property rights in the API Specification, including the API design, endpoint structure, naming conventions, message formats, request and response structures, workflows, integration patterns, and related documentation remain the exclusive property of Veengu and its Affiliates. Access to the API Specification grants the Customer and its authorized integrators a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable license to use the API solely for integration of Customer systems with the Veengu Platform. No ownership rights are transferred and no other rights are granted. 3. tags.name: Direct transfer
tags.description:
The **Direct Transfer** operation (`DIRECT_TRANSFER`) provides a unified and standardized way to process all authorized transfers between individual and business profiles' accounts through a single API. It consolidates transfers, such as person-to-person, business-to-person, business-to-business, and person-to-business.
Unlike system-level **Account Transfers**, Direct Transfers follow full product rules, authorization, and regulatory controls, including balance checks, limit enforcement, and eligibility validation. The system automatically classifies each transaction into a standardized business subtype (`P2P`, `B2P`, `B2B`, `P2B`, or `OAT` (Own Accounts Transfer)) based on the payer and payee profiles, enabling consistent reporting, compliance handling, and downstream processing. Tenant's and Partner's cannot be a payee or payer for Direct Transfer operation type.

tags.name: Invitation Transfer
tags.description: The Invitation Transfer feature allows a verified wallet user (Sender) to send funds to a phone number not yet linked to an eligible recipient. The system holds the funds in an escrow account and releases them only once the recipient fulfills predefined onboarding and KYC conditions.

post.summary: One-time password via sms challenge generation
post.description:
First step for authentication by one-time password delivered by sms. Can be used both for initial authentication and also for subsequent step-up authentication to access operations requiring aditional confirmation/authentication.

Initial authentication requires only `VeenguApiKey`. For extended authentication (to access protected operations), both `VeenguApiKey` and `AccessToken` are required.

**Step-up authentication:** When a protected operation returns `403 ADDITIONAL_AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED` with `details.correlationToken`, pass that token as `correlationToken` in this request. The SMS will then contain context about the linked operation (e.g. amount and type), and the resulting OTP challenge must be completed via `POST /auth/smsotp/authenticate` to authorise that specific operation.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number to deliver the OTP to. Required for initial authentication. May be omitted for step-up authentication when `correlationToken` is provided, in which case the platform uses the phone number of the authenticated identity.

correlationToken.description: Step-up authentication token. When provided, the SMS OTP challenge is linked to the pending operation identified by this token. Obtain this value from `details.correlationToken` in a `403 ADDITIONAL_AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED` response.

post.description:
One-time password verification.

Initial authentication requires only `VeenguApiKey`. For extended authentication (to access protected operations), both `VeenguApiKey` and `AccessToken` are required.

post.description:
Authentication by secret word.

**Step-up authentication:** When a protected operation returns `403 ADDITIONAL_AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED` with `details.correlationToken` and `mfa` containing `SECRET_WORD`, pass that token as `correlationToken` alongside the `secretWord` to authorise that specific pending operation.

put.description: As for now we allow only one key for one identity. Multiple keys may be allowed lately. Only `tweetnacl` key type is supported.

tag.description: The purpose of the authentication key

delete.description: Upon logout the mobile application calls this to revoke the device associated with the given authentication key ID, along with all related keys. Always returns 200, even if the key is not found.

get.summary: The list of user identity defined by phone number or/and email.
get.description:
User can have several identities, for example, user has individual wallet and employee account in business wallet.

When the current session was established via key-based authentication, the response includes a `currentDevice` field describing the device used for this session.

404.description: Identity not found

get.description: Returns all non-revoked devices registered for the current identity. The device associated with the current session is indicated by `isCurrent: true`.

delete.description: Revokes a device by its ID, invalidating all associated authentication keys and sessions. The current device cannot be revoked via this endpoint — use `DELETE /auth/key/{id}` instead.

404.description: Device not found

post.description:
Activates a `PENDING` device.

If the current session contains an SMS OTP correlation token linked to this device, the device is activated
immediately and `{"status": "ACTIVE"}` is returned.

Otherwise, step-up SMS OTP authentication is required: a `403` response is returned containing a
`correlationToken` that must be passed to the SMS OTP challenge flow. Once OTP authentication
completes, retry this endpoint — the device will be activated immediately.

Cannot be called from a session authenticated by a `PENDING` device.

400.description: Bad request. Either the current device is in `PENDING` status and cannot activate other devices, or the target device is not in `PENDING` status.

correlationToken.description: Pass this token as `correlationToken` in the SMS OTP challenge, then retry activation

404.description: Device not found

post.summary: Propose email address for individual profile contact
post.description:
First step of two-step flow to add or edit individual profile EMAIL contact. The back-end generates a random OTP code and sends it to the provided email address. The OTP code must then be submitted via `POST /individuals/{id}/contacts/email/verify` to complete the contact update.
Available regardless of the profile's KYC level.

200.description: OTP code dispatched to the provided email address

400.description: Invalid request. Possible `code` values: `PARAMETER_MISSING` — `email` is missing; `PARAMETER_INVALID` — `email` is empty or has an invalid format; `JSON_INVALID` — malformed JSON body.

403.description: Caller has no access to the target profile (`code` `NOT_AUTHORIZED`)

404.description: Individual profile not found (`code` `RESOURCE_MISSING`)

post.summary: Confirm email address for individual profile contact
post.description:
Second step of two-step flow to add or edit individual profile EMAIL contact. Submits the OTP code received by email. If the code is correct, the back-end replaces the profile's EMAIL contact with the proposed email address. If the profile's EMAIL contact already matches the proposed value, the operation is a no-op.
Available regardless of the profile's KYC level.

200.description: EMAIL contact updated (or kept unchanged if it already matched)

400.description: Invalid request. Possible `code` values: `PARAMETER_MISSING` — `challengeToken` or `otp` is missing; `PARAMETER_INVALID` — `challengeToken` or `otp` is empty; `JSON_INVALID` — malformed JSON body; `WRONG_ONE_TIME_CODE` — the OTP does not match (`message`: `Email update has failed. Wrong one-time code`); `TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS` — the challenge is locked after too many wrong attempts (`message`: `Email update has failed. Wrong one-time code. Too many attempts`); `BAD_CHALLENGE` — the challenge token is invalid or expired (`message`: `Email update has failed. Bad challenge`).

403.description: Caller has no access to the target profile (`code` `NOT_AUTHORIZED`)

404.description: Individual profile not found (`code` `RESOURCE_MISSING`)

post.summary: Invite an individual by phone number
post.description: Creates a new "Invite a Friend" invitation for the specified phone number. The inviter is identified by the authenticated profile (`v-profile`).

200.description: Invitation has been created successfully. The response body is empty.

400.description:
Request is invalid. Following options are possible:

- The invitee phone number is already linked to a registered individual customer. \
  Error `type`=`INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR`, `code`=`INVITATION_NOT_APPLICABLE`

- An active (non-expired) invitation already exists for the invitee phone number within the solution. \
  Error `type`=`INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR`, `code`=`RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS`

- A configured invitation limit has been reached (per invitee per month, or per inviter per day). \
  Error `type`=`INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR`, `code`=`INVITATION_LIMIT_REACHED`,
  `details`=`{ "limitType": "INVITEE_PER_MONTH" | "INVITER_PER_DAY" }`

- The invitee phone number does not match any of the prefixes configured for the solution's invitation feature. \
  Error `type`=`INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR`, `code`=`PARAMETER_INVALID`

get.summary: Get a list of payment groups
get.description:
Retrieves a list of payment groups. Each group contains a list of payment/payout channels defined as payment services. Groups could be organized into hierarchy of payment services menu: each group could has a parent defined.

For the case of intenational remittances countries are usually defined as payment groups: alpha-2 country codes are used as groups IDs to simplify naming and country list filtration.
Retrieves the specific group by its ID.

get.summary: Get a list of payment services
get.description:
Retrieves a list of available payment/payout channels that is defined as a list of payment services. 

Cursor-based pagination is used to fetch payment services. See `next` parameter description for mode details.

To get the list for a specific group use the filter parameter `group`.

parameters.name: V-Version
parameters.description:
API version. Controls the response schema for payment services:
- If >= 3.0.4: new payment service schema is returned with structured `amount` (containing `mode`, `single`, `beneficiary`, `remitter`), top-level `beneficiary` configuration, and deprecated legacy fields (`amountMode`, `senderAmount`, `deal.beneficiary`) returned as null.
- If < 3.0.4 or missing: legacy payment service schema is returned with flat `amount` map, `amountMode`, `senderAmount`, and `deal.beneficiary` fields.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Payment group ID to filter services by. If provided then only services of this group are returned.

parameters.name: next
parameters.description:
Cursor/limit-based pagination is supported to fetch payment services. For each returned page the `next` field in response is a unique key identifying the page next to the page currently returned. Use the `next` string from the response in subsequent request to point to the next page.

If the `next` parameter is not presented in request the first in alphabetical order services are returned in the response.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of rows on one page to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

cursors.description:
`next` and `prev` cursors for page-based navigation across the list of payment services

If all services matching the search criteria are returned in single response then `cursors` field could be missing.

prev.description: Cursor pointing to the previous page of payment services.

next.description: Cursor pointing to the next page of payment services. This value should be used in `next` parameter value of subsequent 'get services' request for navigation to the next page.

type.description:
Error type defines the area to which the error belongs. Following values could be used:

`ACCESS_ERROR` — authentication or authorization error

`INVALID_REQUEST_ERROR` — request is malformed or some provided parameters are invalid

`FINANCIAL_TRANSACTION_ERROR` — request processing has failed due to some financial reasons: not enough founds, balance limits reached and so on

`OTHER_ERROR` — other error that doesn't fit into any category above

code.description:
Error code indicating specific error reason. Following codes should be used:

**Access errors** \
`NOT_AUTHORIZED` — request is authenticated but access is not granted \
`NOT_AUTHENTICATED` — request is not authenticated \
`LEGAL_CONSENT_REQUIRED` — required legal consent is not accepted by the profile; details include `id`, `mandatory`, `requestTitle`, `requestBody`, `url`

**Invalid request errors** \
`PARAMETER_MISSING` — some mandatory parameter/property is missing in request \
`PARAMETER_INVALID` — some parameter/property in request has an invalid value \
`RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS` — unable to create new resource (payment for example) as it already exists \
`RESOURCE_MISSING` — requested resource (payment for example) is missing \
`RESOURCE_NOT_AVAILABLE` — requested resource exists but not available for operation

**Financial transaction errors** \
`CURRENCY_NOT_SUPPORTED` — amount currency is not supported \
`LIMIT_REACHED` — limit has been reached: min/max operation amount bounds exceeded and so on \
`NOT_SUFFICIENT_FUNDS` — operation is not allowed as source account/deposit has not enough funds \
`PARTY_NOT_AVAILABLE` — requested party (beneficiary for example) is not available for operation (blocked for example)\
`OPERATION_NOT_APPLICABLE` — requested operation is not applicable for selected party \
`PAYMENT_INSTRUMENT_UNAVAILABLE` — requested payment instrument is not available for operation

**Other errors** \
`NOT_FOUND` — requested resource/endpoint is not found \
`OTHER_CLIENT_ERROR` — any other client error \
`OTHER_SERVER_ERROR` — any other server error

transaction.description:
On the Veengu ecosystem, transactions are represented by the transaction object.  The attributes of a given transaction object depend on the operation type. Some attributes are required for all transaction objects, such as:
operationType – Categorizes the financial business operation. For the complete list of types, see the "Operation Types" section.
operationSubtype — Optional operation subtype. For some financial operations it is possible to specify subtype when consent is created. Subtype acts as a modifier allowing to use custom fees or process transaction differently.
status – The current status of the transaction. This is the only field that can change after the transaction object has been created.
amount – Amount of the transaction. The float number of monetary units specified in an appropriate currency. Field pattern  ^\d{1,13}\.\d{1,2}$
Each transaction is associated with two accounts that participate in the transaction. The accounts owners can be an individual or a business.

directTransferTransaction_schema.description:
On the Veengu ecosystem, transactions are represented by the transaction object.  The attributes of a given transaction object depend on the operation type. Some attributes are required for all transaction objects, such as:
operationType – Categorizes the financial business operation. For the complete list of types, see the "Operation Types" section.
operationSubtype — Optional operation subtype. For some financial operations it is possible to specify subtype when consent is created. Subtype acts as a modifier allowing to use custom fees or process transaction differently.
status – The current status of the transaction. This is the only field that can change after the transaction object has been created.
amount – Amount of the transaction. The float number of monetary units specified in an appropriate currency. Field pattern  ^\d{1,13}\.\d{1,2}$
Each transaction is associated with two accounts that participate in the transaction. The accounts owners can be an individual or a business.

amount.description:
Payment amount configuration. Response format depends on `V-Version` header:
- V-Version >= 3.0.4: contains structured amount guidelines with `mode` and separate guidelines for `single`, `beneficiary`, and `remitter` currencies.
- V-Version < 3.0.4 or missing: flat map of currency alpha-3 code → amount guidelines (legacy format).

mode.description:
Amount input mode.
- `FIXED` — only fixed amount could be used. Amount value is returned in `/payments/check` response.
- `SUGGESTED` — suggested amount is returned in `/payments/check` response. Any amount could be used.
- `DEFAULT` — no amount is demanded/suggested by the Platform.
- `BENEFICIARY_REMITTER` — separate amounts for beneficiary and remitter currencies.

single.description: Amount guidelines for single-currency payments. Currency alpha-3 code is used as key.

denomination.description: Allowed amount denomination indicating number of digits after the decimal separator.

hint.description: Optional textual human-readable hint for payment amount
hint.example: The amount will be rounded to multiple 10. The amount shall be less than 5000 ZAR

beneficiary.description: Amount guidelines for beneficiary currency. Currency alpha-3 code is used as key.

hint.example: The amount will be rounded to multiple 10. The amount shall be less than 5000 ZAR

remitter.description: Amount guidelines for remitter currency. Currency alpha-3 code is used as key.

hint.example: The amount will be rounded to multiple 10. The amount shall be less than 5000 ZAR

senderAmount.description: Remitter amount guidelines. Deprecated since V-Version 3.0.4, returns null. Use `amount.remitter` instead.

denomination.description: Allowed amount denomination indicating number of digits after the decimal separator.

hint.description: Optional textual human-readable hint for payment amount
hint.example: The amount will be rounded to multiple 10. The amount shall be less than 5000 ZAR

amountMode.description:
Payment amount mode. Deprecated since V-Version 3.0.4, returns null. Use `amount.mode` instead.
- `FIXED` — only fixed amount could be used. Amount value is returned in `/payments/check` response.
- `SUGGESTED` — suggested amount is returned in `/payments/check` response. Any amount could be used.
- `DEFAULT` — no amount is demanded/suggested by the Platform.
- `BENEFICIARY_REMITTER` — separate amounts for beneficiary and remitter currencies.
